pages.raw_text:
The Peerless Employment agency
duct his business by the city comp
troller and Mayor Boyd this morning
In the face of the sensational expose
of The Press Of few days ago. Mr..
Sebring Of this agency was sent to
the penitentiary for one year for try
ing to sell Spokane girls into house
Of Ill fame a few days ago.


Investigation into the matter re-
veals a deplorable state af affairs and
shows where the city ordinance in
this matter is sorely lacking.


An employment agent does not have
to place his application before the
city council for the approval of that
body. He simply files his bond Of
$1,000, has 1t approved by the cor
poration counsel and then places the
thing before the mayor for his APP
proval.


Mayor Boyd approved the Peerless
license this morning and thereby
placed himself on record. He was
seen regarding the matter by a Press
reporter this morning and in explain
ing said that after the bond had
been fled 1t was UP to him to approve
them, as in so doing he was not con-
sidering the desirability of the appli-
cant, but of his bondsmen.


The mayor showed a lack of knowl-
edge of the city ordinances, as he did
not know whether the matter should
be acted upon by the city council or
not. In fact, he said he thought the
matter went from his Office to the
city council for its approval.


Neither is 1t the business of the
city comptroller to pass on the desir-
ability OF the applicant In issuing the
license after the bonds have been APP
proved by the corporation counsers
office. Therefore. a license was rail-
roaded to Triplett this morning,


But Mayor Boyd says that the
council may at any time revoke the
license.


The vital part of the bond reads
that said license Is granted on con-
dition that the applicant shall not
violate any state law or ordinance Of
the city, especially those ordinances
relating to employment or intelli-
yence offices during the term of the
license, and he shall pay all judg
ments rendered against him or all
judgments rendered against the city
by reason Of his willfully deceiving
any person transacting any business
with him as keeper of an employment
agency.


The bond was approved by Assist
ant Corporation Counsel Kenyon and
was signed by Mayor Boyd.
